entries.title: Bands the modem actually advertises
entries.summary: The end result: config ∩ policy. If a band you configured is missing here, carrier policy silently dropped it.

entries.title: Configured 5G NSA bands
entries.summary: The NSA allowlist — separate from SA, and easy to forget.

entries.title: Configured 5G SA bands
entries.summary: Your configured SA allowlist, colon-separated. A lone 0 means EMPTY, not all.

entries.title: Configured LTE bands
entries.summary: The LTE allowlist. On this box two configured bands (7, 38) are silently dropped by carrier policy — compare with policy_band.

entries.title: Preferred network mode
entries.summary: AUTO, NR5G, LTE, or a colon-combination.

entries.title: Set the 5G SA allowlist
entries.summary: Restrict the modem to these SA bands.
entries.warn: Writes NV immediately — there is no commit step; it survives reboot, reflash and factory reset. GL re-applies its own stored config on cellular_manager restart, so prefer the Bands tab (confirm-or-revert + durable) over this raw form. Lock onto a band with no coverage and you lose the link.

params.name: bands
params.hint: Colon-separated band numbers, e.g. 41:66:71. Only policy-permitted bands ever take (see policy_band).

entries.title: Which bands the carrier permits
entries.summary: The carrier's per-SIM allowlist — the list no router UI shows you. What the modem actually uses is your config ∩ this. On this box T-Mobile permits 6 of the 18 SA bands the module supports.

entries.title: Cell-lock persistence flags
entries.summary: Two 0/1 flags controlling whether cell locks persist (exact mapping unconfirmed; this box reads 0,0). Unlike band config, cell lock appears to have a non-persist option.

entries.title: Clear a cell lock
entries.summary: Release the LTE cell lock and let the modem roam again — the other half of any lock experiment.
entries.warn: Set-side UNVERIFIED. The query form returns ",0" when unlocked, so ",0" to clear is plausible — confirm on a box you can reach out-of-band before relying on it.
entries.source: inferred from the query form — unverified

entries.title: LTE cell-lock status
entries.summary: Query the LTE cell lock. ",0" means not locked.

entries.title: Lock to a 5G cell
entries.summary: Pin the modem to one NR cell. PCI comes FIRST — an earlier community guess had it second, which would lock to a nonexistent cell.
entries.warn: Set-side semantics are NOT fully mapped (scs encoding, persistence). If that cell goes away, so does your connection. Syntax is from the box's own test form; nobody has verified a successful set yet.
entries.source: AT+QNWLOCK=? test form captured on box 2026-07-17 (reference §6a)

params.name: pci
params.hint: Physical cell ID of the target cell

params.name: scs
params.hint: Subcarrier spacing — encoding not yet mapped (likely an index for 15/30/60 kHz)

params.name: band
params.hint: NR band number, without the n prefix

entries.title: Lock to an LTE cell
entries.summary: Pin the modem to one LTE cell: mode, then EARFCN, then PCI.
entries.warn: Set-side semantics are NOT fully mapped on the RG650V (mode 0-10 meaning, persistence vs save_ctrl). If that cell goes away, so does your connection. Syntax is from the box's own test form; nobody has verified a successful set yet.
entries.source: AT+QNWLOCK=? test form captured on box 2026-07-17 (reference §6a)

params.name: mode
params.hint: Lock strength 0-10 — exact meaning not yet mapped on this module

params.name: earfcn
params.hint: EARFCN of the target cell (from the serving-cell/neighbour reads)

params.name: pci
params.hint: Physical cell ID of the target cell

entries.title: NR cell-lock status
entries.summary: Query the NR cell lock. ",0" means not locked. Note: a band lock (QNWPREFCFG) is a different mechanism and won't show here.

entries.title: 5G network registration
entries.summary: Whether you're registered on 5G. Second number: 1 = home, 5 = roaming, 2 = searching, 3 = denied.

entries.title: Carrier aggregation
entries.summary: Which carriers are aggregated right now. No field decode yet: this box returns pcell_state=5, which the (5-series) manual says can't happen — don't trust positional decodes until that's resolved.
entries.source: manual §5.13; pcell_state anomaly captured on box 2026-07-17

entries.title: Modem firmware version
entries.summary: Full firmware build string. Quote this when reporting AT behaviour — AT command sets are firmware-specific.

entries.title: Modem identity
entries.summary: Manufacturer, model and revision. The universal 'what am I talking to'.

entries.title: Operator name (from the SIM)
entries.summary: Service provider name as the SIM reports it — not what the tower advertises. The last field is the registered PLMN.

entries.title: Serving cell details
entries.summary: Everything about the cell you're camped on — PCI, ARFCN, band, and the three numbers that matter (RSRP, RSRQ, SINR).
entries.source: RG50xQ&RM5xxQ manual §5.11 format; NR5G-SA field order verified on box 2026-07-17

entries.title: Signal quality (universal)
entries.summary: The one signal read every modem ever made understands. RSSI 0-31 (99 = unknown), bit error rate.

entries.title: Persist the change — SIM1 (QPRTPARA)
entries.summary: Save/back up modem NV so a SIM1 change (e.g. an IMEI write) survives reboot. The '1' selects the backup action; QPRTPARA=? on this box returns (1-4).
entries.warn: Action, not a query — writes NAND. The 1-4 argument mapping is not fully verified on the RG650V (community and a different-family app note both read =1 as the save/back-up step). Do not power off mid-execute; don't run it frequently (NAND wear).
entries.source: GL.iNet forum 'Mudi 7 Mega Post', 2026-07-18; QPRTPARA=? range seen on box (reference §5)

entries.title: Persist the change — SIM2 / eSIM (QEFSSYNC)
entries.summary: The save step for SIM2 / eSIM changes — the community reports this is the correct sequence for slot 2, distinct from SIM1's QPRTPARA.
entries.warn: Action that flushes the modem's EFS to NV. Unverified on the RG650V; argument 0 per community report. Use this (not QPRTPARA) after an EGMR write with index 1,11 (SIM2/eSIM).

entries.title: Set the IMEI (SIM1)
entries.summary: Write the IMEI for SIM1's subscription (index 1,7). A community IMEI-repair step — must be followed by a save (QPRTPARA=1) and a reboot, or it won't stick.
entries.warn: Writes device identity to NV (survives reboot/factory reset). Changing an IMEI is LEGALLY RESTRICTED in many countries — only for restoring your own device's IMEI. Unverified on the RG650V; 1,7 = SIM1, 1,11 = SIM2/eSIM per community reports. Copy your current IMEI first: it is lost if you don't save it.

params.name: imei
params.hint: The 15-digit IMEI to write (keep the quotes). This form targets SIM1 (1,7); for SIM2/eSIM use index 1,11.

entries.title: Radio off / on
entries.summary: Airplane mode at the modem. 0 is off, 1 is full function. Forces re-registration on the way back up.
entries.warn: Runtime only — a reboot restores the radio — but you WILL drop the cellular link (and possibly this admin session) for ~20 seconds.
